---
title: "DGG Grid System"
engine: julia
---

# DGG Grid System

The DGG (Discrete Global Grid) module provides parametric grid types inspired by [DGGRID](https://github.com/sahrk/DGGRID).  Grid configuration is encoded in type parameters, enabling zero-cost dispatch across different projections, apertures, and topologies.

## Grid Configuration

A DGG grid is parameterized by three values:

| Parameter | Options | Description |
|---|---|---|
| **Projection** | `:isea`, `:fuller` | Map projection (currently uses Lambert azimuthal equal-area) |
| **Aperture** | `3`, `4`, `7`, `43` | Number of child cells per parent (currently `3` is implemented) |
| **Topology** | `:hex`, `:tri`, `:diamond` | Cell shape (currently `:hex` is implemented) |

This gives **12 preset grid types**, each with a corresponding cell type:

| Grid | Cell | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `ISEA3H` | `ISEA3HCell` | ISEA aperture-3 hexagonal |
| `ISEA4H` | `ISEA4HCell` | ISEA aperture-4 hexagonal |
| `ISEA7H` | `ISEA7HCell` | ISEA aperture-7 hexagonal |
| `ISEA43H` | `ISEA43HCell` | ISEA mixed 4-3 hexagonal |
| `ISEA4T` | `ISEA4TCell` | ISEA aperture-4 triangular |
| `ISEA4D` | `ISEA4DCell` | ISEA aperture-4 diamond |
| `FULLER3H` | `FULLER3HCell` | Fuller aperture-3 hexagonal |
| `FULLER4H` | `FULLER4HCell` | Fuller aperture-4 hexagonal |
| `FULLER7H` | `FULLER7HCell` | Fuller aperture-7 hexagonal |
| `FULLER43H` | `FULLER43HCell` | Fuller mixed 4-3 hexagonal |
| `FULLER4T` | `FULLER4TCell` | Fuller aperture-4 triangular |
| `FULLER4D` | `FULLER4DCell` | Fuller aperture-4 diamond |

::: {.callout-note}
Currently only **aperture-3 hexagonal** grids (`ISEA3H`, `FULLER3H`) are fully implemented.  Other apertures and topologies are defined in the type system but not yet functional.
:::

## Cell Index Layout

Each cell wraps a single `UInt64` encoding the base vertex (0â€“11) and hierarchical digits:

| Aperture | Bits/digit | Max digits | Max resolution |
|---|---|---|---|
| 3, 4 | 2 | 28 | 28 |
| 7, 43 | 3 | 20 | 20 |

**Cell count** at resolution $r$ with aperture $A$: $\;10 \cdot A^r + 2$

## Creating a DGGCell

### From Coordinates and Resolution

```julia
using GlobalGrids

coord = LonLat(-75.0, 54.0)
cell = ISEA3HCell(coord, 5)
```

### From Base Vertex and Digits

```julia
# Base vertex 0, digits [1, 2, 0]
cell = ISEA3HCell(0, [1, 2, 0])
```

### From Hex String

```julia
cell = ISEA3HCell(coord, 5)
str = GlobalGrids.dgg_string(cell)
ISEA3HCell(str)
```

### Using the Convenience Wrapper

```julia
# dggcells defaults to ISEA3H
dggcells(LonLat(0.0, 0.0), 5)

# Specify a different grid
dggcells(LonLat(0.0, 0.0), 5; grid=FULLER3H())
```

## Inspecting Cells

```julia
using GlobalGrids

cell = ISEA3HCell(LonLat(-75.0, 54.0), 5)

GlobalGrids.resolution(cell)                     # 5
GlobalGrids.is_pentagon(cell)                     # false
GlobalGrids.dgg_digits(cell.index, Val(3))        # digit vector
GlobalGrids.decode(cell)                          # "3-12010" (base-digits)
```

## GeoInterface

```julia
import GeoInterface as GI

cell = ISEA3HCell(LonLat(-75.0, 54.0), 5)

GI.centroid(cell)       # center LonLat
GI.coordinates(cell)    # boundary vertices (7 for hex, 6 for pentagon â€” closed)
GI.area(cell)           # area in mÂ˛
```

## Grid Hierarchy

```julia
using GlobalGrids

cell = ISEA3HCell(LonLat(0.0, 0.0), 3)

p = GlobalGrids.parent(cell)        # resolution 2 parent
ch = GlobalGrids.children(cell)     # 3 children per hex
sib = GlobalGrids.siblings(cell)    # same-parent siblings
```

Pentagons at any resolution:

```julia
pents = GlobalGrids.dgg_pentagons(ISEA3H(), 3)  # 12 pentagons at resolution 3
```

## Quantizing Geometries

```julia
using GlobalGrids
import GeoInterface as GI

# Point
dggcells(LonLat(0.0, 0.0), 5)

# Line
dggcells(GI.Line([(0.0, 0.0), (5.0, 5.0)]), 3; containment=:center)

# Polygon
boundary = [(-20.0, -20.0), (-20.0, 20.0), (20.0, 20.0), (20.0, -20.0), (-20.0, -20.0)]
dggcells(GI.Polygon([boundary]), 2; containment=:center)
```

## Comparison with IGEO7

Both DGG (aperture-3) and IGEO7 (aperture-7) use Lambert azimuthal equal-area projection centered on icosahedron vertices.  Key differences:

| | ISEA3H | IGEO7 |
|---|---|---|
| Aperture | 3 | 7 |
| Children per hex | 3 | 7 |
| Children per pentagon | 3 | 6 |
| Digits | 0â€“2 | 0â€“6 |
| Rotation per level | 30Â° | 19.1Â° |
| Max resolution | 28 | 20 |
| Cells at res 5 | 2,432 | 168,072 |
